# Poor Paul's Benchmark (PPB)
# https://github.com/paulplee/poor-pauls-benchmark

## What This Is

Poor Paul's Benchmark is an automated local LLM inference benchmarking tool.
It measures GPU performance across models, quantizations, and context sizes,
then publishes results to a public HuggingFace dataset.

## What It Produces

Every benchmark run outputs a JSONL record containing:
- GPU hardware profile (name, VRAM, driver version)
- Model identity (HuggingFace repo, filename, quantization label)
- Benchmark parameters (context size, batch size, concurrent users)
- Performance metrics (throughput tokens/sec, TTFT ms, ITL ms)
- System metadata (OS, CPU, RAM, LLM engine version)

The full schema is documented at: docs/schema.md

## Where Results Go

Results are published to:
  Dataset:    https://huggingface.co/datasets/paulplee/ppb-results
  Analytics:  https://poorpaul.dev/insights
  MCP server: https://mcp.poorpaul.dev (queryable by LLM clients)

## Key Commands

  ppb hw-info                        # show hardware profile
  ppb vram-cliff suites/my_gpu.toml  # find max safe context window
  ppb sweep      suites/my_gpu.toml  # run parameter sweep
  ppb publish    --results r.jsonl   # publish to HuggingFace
  ppb all        suites/my_gpu.toml  # vram-cliff + sweep + publish

## Suite Configuration

Benchmarks are configured in TOML files (see suites/suite.example.toml).
Key parameters: repo_id, filename, runner_type, n_ctx, n_batch, concurrent_users.

## Related Projects

  ppb-mcp:   https://github.com/paulplee/ppb-mcp
  Dataset:   https://huggingface.co/datasets/paulplee/ppb-results
  Website:   https://poorpaul.dev
